text:
September 10th: Part 4 (910④, Kugatsu Tōka (Yon)) is the 127 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

Gon enters Greed Island's Starting Point and walks down a hallway, eventually meeting a woman sitting in a floating mechanism and wearing headgear. She immediately welcomes him to Greed Island and asks if he is Gon, which he confirms. Before they start, the woman tells Gon that Ging has left a voice message for him. Ging congratulates Gon and tells him that he is in a game, he created with his friends, adding that Gon shouldn't expect any clues to find his location and that he just wanted to show off the game they made together. Ging also tells him that he won't be able to leave the game until he finds a special item, and Gon wonders if the only thing Ging wanted to do was show off the game.

After Gon hears his father's message, the woman starts to explain the rules, telling him that a player who has the special ring can use two spells: "Book" and "Gain." She tells him to hold out his hand with the ring and to say "Book," which causes a floating book to appear in front of Gon. She explains that he can convert every item he finds into a card and that the cards are stored in the book, adding that he will need to complete it to clear the game.

She then asks Gon to open his book to the first page, and he sees empty slots with numbers on each of them. The woman explains that each slot will only accept that specific card. As Gon keeps turning the pages and finds slots with no numbers, she explains that those are called "free slots" and can hold cards of any number, adding that there are 100 specific slots (000 to 099) and 45 free slots. She concludes this part of her explanation by saying that to clear the game, Gon will need to collect all 100 specified slot cards.

The woman clarifies that he will be collecting 100 items and turning them into cards, explaining that if he finds a sword and touches it, it will immediately turn into a card. She adds that if he puts the card in his book, he won't be able to use it as a weapon unless he uses the spell "Gain." However, she explains that once a card has been "gained," it can never be turned into a card again, which would force a player to find the same sword again if he wanted the card. With Gon just barely following along, she explains that there are two other times when an item cannot be turned into a card. The first is when the item has reached its conversion limit, which can range from as few as three to more than 100. She gives an example of an item with a limit of three cards, explaining that if three players each have one in their binder, a fourth player cannot convert it into an item when he finds it. The second is that cards must be kept in the book and that if a card is left out for 60 seconds, it reverts into the item and can never be converted into a card again.

As Gon begins to reach his memorization limit, she stresses that if a player dies, their book and ring will be destroyed, and all the cards inside will disappear. She says that these are only the basic rules and that he will have to discover more details by playing the game, finally wishing him good luck and showing him the stairs that lead down below. Gon walks down the stairs and finds himself looking out across a huge, grassy plain. He then senses that something is watching him, but doesn't see any sign of people. Another player enters the game and walks past Gon, and two other players meet up and also sense that they're being watched, with one realizing that it must be a human and adding that they must not be any good since they're being so obvious. As the two make their way across the plain, Gon is filled with excitement as he begins to play the game his father made.
